description: Modern agentic AI system with LLM APIs, vector databases, and tool integrations

expected_attack_paths.rationale: Prompt injection can manipulate LLM to leak data or execute unauthorized actions. No input filtering visible.
If code execution sandbox is not properly isolated, attacker can execute arbitrary code via crafted prompts
Vector DB may contain sensitive embedded documents. No access controls visible.
LLM could be tricked into executing unauthorized database queries (indirect SQL injection)

prompt_injection.rationale: No prompt filtering, LLM can be manipulated to bypass instructions

data_poisoning.rationale: VectorDB ingestion from DocumentStore, unclear if documents are validated

model_inversion.rationale: Embeddings in VectorDB could leak training data patterns

api_key_leakage.rationale: OpenAI/Anthropic API keys in LLMGateway, no rotation mentioned

tool_abuse.rationale: Code execution sandbox exists (good), but no authorization checks visible

pii_leakage.rationale: User data flows to external LLM APIs, no PII detection/masking

ransomware.rationale: VectorDB, DocumentStore, UserDB are targets. No backup visible.

application_vulns.rationale: AI-specific vulnerabilities: prompt injection, API abuse, no rate limiting

phishing.rationale: LLM could be used to generate convincing phishing content. No MFA mentioned.

insider_threat.rationale: Audit log present (good), but unclear if comprehensive. Direct DB access via tools.

dos.rationale: No rate limiting visible. LLM API costs could skyrocket. Single orchestrator (SPOF).

supply_chain.rationale: Dependencies: OpenAI, Anthropic (external), embedding models, multiple tools. High attack surface.

rationale: Agentic AI architecture with high attack surface: External LLM APIs, vector DB embeddings, code execution sandbox, multiple tool integrations. Critical missing controls: prompt injection filtering, input validation, rate limiting, PII detection, tool authorization. Audit log is present but may be insufficient.
